This species belongs to the genus Rhapis, which consists of several species of rhizomatous palms. Rhapis gryllus is a type of fan palm native to East Asia. Its classification is further supported by its morphological characteristics, such as its fan-shaped leaves and woody stems. As a member of the Arecaceae family, Rhaphis gryllus shares similarities with other palm species in terms of its growth habits and leaf morphology.
